mission.title: signoz: [Dashboard Request] ASP .NET Metrics Dashboard
mission.description: Create an ASP.NET Metrics Dashboard in SigNoz to monitor key application metrics like request rate, error rate, latency, and resource utilization for .NET applications instrumented with OpenTelemetry.

steps.title: Instrument ASP.NET application with OpenTelemetry
steps.description:
Add the OpenTelemetry SDK NuGet packages to your ASP.NET application and configure the exporter to send metrics to SigNoz:
```bash
dotnet add package OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting
dotnet add package OpenTelemetry.Instrumentation.AspNetCore
dotnet add package OpenTelemetry.Instrumentation.Runtime
dotnet add package OpenTelemetry.Exporter.OpenTelemetryProtocol
```
Then configure OpenTelemetry in your Program.cs (see resolution.codeSnippets for example configuration).

steps.title: Verify metrics are being received in SigNoz
steps.description:
Check the SigNoz UI to confirm that metrics are flowing from your ASP.NET application:
```bash
# Port-forward to SigNoz frontend (if running in Kubernetes)
kubectl port-forward -n signoz svc/signoz-frontend 3301:3301
```
Navigate to http://localhost:3301 and go to Services → Your App Name to verify metrics like http_server_request_duration are being received.

steps.title: Create the ASP.NET Metrics Dashboard
steps.description:
In the SigNoz UI, create a new dashboard with panels for key HTTP metrics:

1. **Request Rate Panel**: Use ClickHouse query against http_server_request_duration metric with rate aggregation
2. **Error Rate Panel**: Query http_server_request_duration filtered by http.status_code >= 400
3. **P99 Latency Panel**: Use P99 quantile of http_server_request_duration
4. **Active Requests Panel**: Display current http_server_active_requests count

Each panel should use the OTLP metric naming conventions and can be filtered by attributes like http.method, http.route, or http.status_code.

steps.title: Add resource utilization panels
steps.description:
Enhance the dashboard with .NET runtime metrics for comprehensive monitoring:

1. **GC Collections Panel**: Track process_runtime_dotnet_gc_collections by generation (gen0, gen1, gen2)
2. **ThreadPool Metrics Panel**: Monitor process_runtime_dotnet_threadpool_* metrics
3. **Memory Usage Panel**: Display process_runtime_dotnet_gc_heap_size and process_runtime_dotnet_gc_allocated_bytes
4. **CPU Usage Panel**: Show process.runtime.dotnet.cpu.utilization

These metrics are automatically collected when using OpenTelemetry.Instrumentation.Runtime.

steps.title: Verify dashboard displays data correctly
steps.description:
Confirm all dashboard panels are showing data:
```bash
# Generate some test traffic to your ASP.NET application
curl http://localhost:5000/api/health
curl http://localhost:5000/api/users
```
Refresh the SigNoz dashboard and verify that all panels update with the new metrics. Check that time ranges, filters, and aggregations work as expected.

resolution.summary: Created an ASP.NET Metrics Dashboard in SigNoz by instrumenting the application with OpenTelemetry .NET SDK and configuring dashboard panels for HTTP request metrics (rate, errors, latency) and .NET runtime metrics (GC, ThreadPool, memory). The dashboard uses ClickHouse queries against OpenTelemetry metric names like http_server_request_duration, http_server_active_requests, and process_runtime_dotnet_gc_collections.

codeSnippets.description: Configure OpenTelemetry metrics instrumentation in ASP.NET Core application

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with signoz installed or the issue environment reproducible.
